data: Fill blood up exactly to the 2 mL level as indicated on the blood tube and mix well.
Arterial puncture and blood from CVP catheters are not suitable.
It is important to draw venous blood from a clean venepuncture into a dry syringe uncontaminated with heparin, intravenous fluids, or other foreign substances.
Haemolyzed sample is unsuitable for coagulation studies
Specimen must be received within 4 hours from time of specimen collection
For assessment of secondary fibrinolysis in suspected venous thrombotic embolism (VTE) and monitoring of post-operative thrombotic risk.

Elevated levels of D-Dimers, the fibrinolytic degradation products of crosslinked fibrin, are associated with venous thromboembolic disorders such as disseminated intravascular coagulation (DIC), deep venous thrombosis (DVT) and pulmonary embolism (PE).
Increased D-Dimer levels have also be used as a predictive indicator in an acute myocardial infarction.
